perturbation.original_text: 3.7. The Unit prices and monthly License Fees of $[*] can't be increased by WLI under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7. The Unit prices and monthly License Fees of $[*] can't be increased by WLI under the terms of this Agreement, except in cases of significant market inflation exceeding 10% annually.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except in cases of significant market inflation exceeding 10% annually,' a contradiction is created. Previously, the text strictly forbade any price increases. Now, an exception exists, making the enforcement of the price stability clause ambiguous. Any part of the contract referring to fixed prices is now contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7. The Unit prices and monthly License Fees of $[*] can't be increased by WLI under the terms of this Agreement, except in cases of significant market inflation exceeding 10% annually.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits any price increases. The changed text introduces an exception for significant market inflation. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about price stability, therefore it is a strong in-text contradiction as it is conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 10.3. Power2Ship will receive the first customer call and will have to maintain a minimum inventory level of spare Units to service its customers within 48 hours. WLI will support an end-user only upon Power2Ship's request and only after Power2Ship has tried to provide the support and has failed to fix the problem. WLI will not be involved in any end-user training.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.3. Power2Ship will receive the first customer call and will have to make commercially reasonable efforts to maintain a minimum inventory level of spare Units to service its customers within 48 hours. WLI will support an end-user directly without Power2Ship's request and may provide any end-user training at its discretion. WLI will not be involved in any end-user training
perturbation.explanation: The replacement of 'will have to maintain' with 'will have to make commercially reasonable efforts to maintain' weakens Power2Ship's obligation to maintain inventory and the addition of, 'WLI will support an end-user directly without Power2Ship's request and may provide any end-user training at its discretion. WLI will not be involved in any end-user training' directly contradicts the second sentence by stating WLI *will* support end-users directly and might offer training instead of 'only' supporting via Power2Ship's request; thus creating ambiguity about support responsibilities and also, WLI will not be involved in any end-user training. Therefor there's an in-text contradiction in location 10.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.3. Power2Ship will receive the first customer call and will have to make commercially reasonable efforts to maintain a minimum inventory level of spare Units to service its customers within 48 hours. WLI will support an end-user directly without Power2Ship's request and may provide any end-user training at its discretion. WLI will not be involved in any end-user training
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section 10.3. Originally, WLI was to support end-users only upon Power2Ship's request. The modified text states, 'WLI will support an end-user directly without Power2Ship's request,' and 'WLI will not be involved in any end-user training', but also 'WLI may provide any end-user training at its discretion' This is a clear and impactful contradiction regarding the support responsibilities and authorization required, creating ambiguity and making enforcement unclear.
